channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korea says Russia supplied air defense missiles to North Korea in return for its troops
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Russia has supplied air defense missile systems to North Korea in exchange for sending its troops to support Russia's war efforts against Ukraine, a top South Korean official said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Bahamas will refinance part of its debt to protect its famous turquoise waters
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The Bahamas announced Friday that it will refinance $300 million of its external debt to free up more than $120 million for marine conservation projects and climate change mitigation.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Elderly man accused of roommate and dog's 'brutal' murder had extensive criminal record
item.description: Austin police said an elderly man has been arrested for the "very brutal" deadly attack on his roommate and her dog, then using her credit cards and planning to flee the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of woman who went missing on cross-country vacation says cryptic texts sent from phone unlike her
item.description: Hannah Kobayashi never got on her flight from LA to NYC on Nov. 8 and disappeared in Los Angeles; new surveillance footage shows her with a mysterious man, her family says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Perez Hilton says Jussie Smollett decision won't help 'tanked' career: Court didn't dispute hate crime hoax
item.description: Pop culture expert and crime buff Perez Hilton weighs in on the Illinois Supreme Court's decision to toss the hate crime hoax conviction of Jussie Smollett.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indigenous leaders travel to UK from Peru to draw attention to oil damage and banking
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Indigenous leaders from the Wampis Nation in Peru are urging lawmakers at the House of Commons in London to ban international banks' support for Amazon oil activities they say harm their ancestral rainforests.
item.dc:creator: ED DAVEY and STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Murdered Kansas mom suffered 30 stab, cut wounds trying to defend herself: autopsy
item.description: An Oklahoma medical examiner's office has released the full autopsy report for the one of the two Kansas moms found buried in a freezer in a cow pasture earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your paycheck may be larger in 2025, even if you don't get a raise
item.description: While there's plenty of time left in November, you may already have something exciting to look forward to in 2025: a slightly larger paycheck, even without a raise.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KTLA 5

item.title: OpenTable names 2024's 'Top 100 Restaurants' in America
item.description: OpenTable's analysts based their findings on more than 14 million diner reviews, along with diner ratings, reservation demand, and percentage of five-star reviews, among other factors.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, KTLA 5

item.title: Jonathan Majors’ ex-girlfriend drops assault, defamation suit
item.description: The civil suit was prompted by the Marvel and “Creed III” actor’s repeated public denial that he attacked Jabbari.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: How 80-year-old crosswording juror caused a mistrial in Florida Home depot murder case
item.description: After more than five hours of deliberations, a Florida judge declared a mistrial because of problems with a juror who was reportedly working on crossword puzzles during the murder trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canoeist paddling 6,000-mile Great Loop out of gratitude for life
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Peter Frank has paddled from Michigan's Upper Peninsula in June to the Chesapeake Bay in Maryland this month in his 1982 Sawyer Loon decked canoe, but he’s still got a long way to go.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas is taking a final vote on allowing Bible-infused lessons in public schools
item.description: Texas education officials will soon decide if a controversial Bible-infused curriculum can be taught in public schools.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about a Wisconsin man who faked his own death and fled to Eastern Europe
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer and left his wife and three children has been located in Eastern Europe and is communicating with law enforcement, but he has not committed to returning home, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KREX 5

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, NewsNation

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 House Republicans seek to stop IVF expansion in defense bill
item.description: The topic came to the forefront after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade, spurred by social conservatives’ belief that life begins at conception.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: An unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals' food, said scientists hoping to curb the strandings.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: Women will for the first time make up a majority of state legislators in Colorado and New Mexico next year, but at least 13 states saw losses in female representation after the November election, according to a count released Thursday by the Rutgers Center for American Women and Politics.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children has been communicating with authorities daily from Eastern Europe, even telling them how he did it, but has not committed to returning home, a sheriff said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: The nation is set to mark 61 years since President John F. Kennedy was assassinated as his motorcade passed through downtown Dallas on Nov. 22, 1963.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Chicano Squad’s impact on Houston after the Moody Park Riots
item.description: The Moody Riots proved Houston’s Hispanic community had a major trust problem when it came to police. That’s when the Houston Police Department formed a special task force -- The Chicano Squad.
item.dc:creator: Daniella Guzman, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Trump taps Brendan Carr to be FCC chairman
item.description: President-elect Trump is tapping Brendan Carr of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to lead the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
